title: Emperor Murakami

infobox.value: 5 July 967(967-07-05) (aged 42) Seiryōden of the Heian Kyō
[Imperial House of Japan](/wiki/Imperial_House_of_Japan "Imperial House of Japan")

(村上天皇, *Murakami-tennō*; 14 July 924 – 5 July 967) was the 62nd [emperor](/wiki/Emperor "Emperor") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"),[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) according to the traditional [order of succession](/wiki/List_of_Emperors_of_Japan "List of Emperors of Japan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

(*[Tengyō](/wiki/Tengy%C5%8D "Tengyō") 9, 13th day of the 4th month*): In the 16th year of the reign of Emperor Suzaku (朱雀天皇十六年), the emperor abdicated; and the succession (the *senso*) was received by his younger brother, Nariakira-shinnō.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
(*Tengyō 9, 28th day of the 4th month*): Shortly thereafter, Emperor Murakami, who was 21 years old, acceded to the throne (the *sokui*).[[8]](#cite_note-8)
(*[Tenryaku](/wiki/Tenryaku "Tenryaku") 3, 29th day of the 9th month*): The former-Emperor Yōzei died at the age of 82.[[9]](#cite_note-b296-9)

(*Tenryaku 5*): The emperor ordered the compilation of *[Gosen Wakashū](/wiki/Gosen_Wakash%C5%AB "Gosen Wakashū")*; this work was undertaken by the [Five Men of the Pear Chamber](/wiki/Five_Men_of_the_Pear_Chamber "Five Men of the Pear Chamber") under his patronage.
(*Tenryaku 6, 15th day of the 8th month*): The former-Emperor Suzaku died at the age of 30.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

(*[Tentoku](/wiki/Tentoku "Tentoku") 4, 23rd day of the 9th month*): The Imperial palace burned down, the first time it had been ravaged by fire since the capital was removed from [Nara](/wiki/Nara,_Nara "Nara, Nara") to [Heian-kyō](/wiki/Heian-ky%C5%8D "Heian-kyō") in 794.[[11]](#cite_note-b297-11)
(*[Kōhō](/wiki/K%C5%8Dh%C5%8D "Kōhō") 4, 25th day of the 5th month*): The former-Emperor Murakami died at the age of 42.[[11]](#cite_note-b297-11)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Emperor of Japan from 946 to 967

| Emperor Murakami 村上天皇 | |
| --- | --- |

| Reign | 23 May 946 – 5 July 967 |
| [Enthronement](/wiki/Enthronement_of_the_Japanese_emperor "Enthronement of the Japanese emperor") | 31 May 946 |

| Successor | [Reizei](/wiki/Emperor_Reizei "Emperor Reizei") |
|  | |

| Died | 5 July 967(967-07-05) (aged 42) Seiryōden of the Heian Kyō |

| [Issue](/wiki/Issue_(genealogy) "Issue (genealogy)") *[more...](#Consorts_and_children)* | * [Emperor Reizei](/wiki/Emperor_Reizei "Emperor Reizei") * [Emperor En'yū](/wiki/Emperor_En%27y%C5%AB "Emperor En'yū") |
| | [Posthumous name](/wiki/Posthumous_name "Posthumous name") | | | --- | --- | | [*Tsuigō*](/wiki/Posthumous_name#Tsuigō "Posthumous name"): Emperor Murakami (村上天皇) | | | |

| Mother | [Fujiwara no Onshi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Onshi "Fujiwara no Onshi") |

**Emperor Murakami** (村上天皇, *Murakami-tennō*; 14 July 924 – 5 July 967) was the 62nd [emperor](/wiki/Emperor "Emperor") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"),[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) according to the traditional [order of succession](/wiki/List_of_Emperors_of_Japan "List of Emperors of Japan").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Murakami's reign spanned the years from [946 to his death in 967](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Murakami&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Before he ascended to the [Chrysanthemum Throne](/wiki/Chrysanthemum_Throne "Chrysanthemum Throne"), his personal name (*[imina](/wiki/Imina "Imina")*) was Nariakira*-shinnō* (成明親王).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Nariakira*-shinnō* was the 14th son of [Emperor Daigo](/wiki/Emperor_Daigo "Emperor Daigo"), and the younger brother of Emperor Suzaku by another mother.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Murakami had ten Empresses and Imperial consorts and 19 Imperial sons and daughters.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Events of Murakami's reign

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Murakami&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Events of  Murakami's reign")]

In 944, he was appointed crown prince and ascended the throne two years later.

* **16 May 946** (*[Tengyō](/wiki/Tengy%C5%8D "Tengyō") 9, 13th day of the 4th month*): In the 16th year of the reign of Emperor Suzaku (朱雀天皇十六年), the emperor abdicated; and the succession (the *senso*) was received by his younger brother, Nariakira-shinnō.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* **31 May 946** (*Tengyō 9, 28th day of the 4th month*): Shortly thereafter, Emperor Murakami, who was 21 years old, acceded to the throne (the *sokui*).[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Murakami's maternal uncle [Fujiwara no Tadahira](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Tadahira "Fujiwara no Tadahira") remained as the *[Sessho](/wiki/Sessho "Sessho")* [regent](/wiki/Regent "Regent") until 949. After the death of Tadahira, there was no regent and although contemporaries praised Murakami as the emperor who governed the state directly, in reality the [Fujiwara clan](/wiki/Fujiwara_clan "Fujiwara clan") seized power and ruled Japan. The brothers [Fujiwara no Saneyori](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Saneyori "Fujiwara no Saneyori") and [Fujiwara no Morosuke](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Morosuke "Fujiwara no Morosuke") became the *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* rulers of Japan.

* **23 October 949** (*[Tenryaku](/wiki/Tenryaku "Tenryaku") 3, 29th day of the 9th month*): The former-Emperor Yōzei died at the age of 82.[[9]](#cite_note-b296-9)
* **951** (*Tenryaku 5*): The emperor ordered the compilation of *[Gosen Wakashū](/wiki/Gosen_Wakash%C5%AB "Gosen Wakashū")*; this work was undertaken by the [Five Men of the Pear Chamber](/wiki/Five_Men_of_the_Pear_Chamber "Five Men of the Pear Chamber") under his patronage.
* **6 September 952** (*Tenryaku 6, 15th day of the 8th month*): The former-Emperor Suzaku died at the age of 30.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* **16 October 960** (*[Tentoku](/wiki/Tentoku "Tentoku") 4, 23rd day of the 9th month*): The Imperial palace burned down, the first time it had been ravaged by fire since the capital was removed from [Nara](/wiki/Nara,_Nara "Nara, Nara") to [Heian-kyō](/wiki/Heian-ky%C5%8D "Heian-kyō") in 794.[[11]](#cite_note-b297-11)

Murakami was a central figure in [Heian period](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period") culture. He was also a skilled [flute](/wiki/Fue_(flute) "Fue (flute)") and [koto](/wiki/Koto_(musical_instrument) "Koto (musical instrument)") (Japanese harp) player.

* **5 July 967** (*[Kōhō](/wiki/K%C5%8Dh%C5%8D "Kōhō") 4, 25th day of the 5th month*): The former-Emperor Murakami died at the age of 42.[[11]](#cite_note-b297-11)

The actual site of Murakami's [grave](/wiki/Grave_(burial) "Grave (burial)") is known.[[1]](#cite_note-kunaicho-1) This emperor is traditionally venerated at a [memorial](/wiki/Memorial "Memorial") [Shinto](/wiki/Shinto "Shinto") [shrine](/wiki/Shrine "Shrine") (*misasagi*) at Kyoto.

The [Imperial Household Agency](/wiki/Imperial_Household_Agency "Imperial Household Agency") designates this location as Murakami's [mausoleum](/wiki/Mausoleum "Mausoleum"). It is formally named *Murakami no misasagi*[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Kugyō

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Murakami&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kugyō")]

*[Kugyō](/wiki/Kugy%C5%8D "Kugyō")* (公卿) is a collective term for the very few most powerful men attached to the court of the [Emperor of Japan](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan") in pre-[Meiji](/wiki/Meiji_period "Meiji period") eras.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In general, this elite group included only three to four men at a time. These were hereditary courtiers whose experience and background have brought them to the pinnacle of a life's career. During Murakami's reign, this apex of the *[Daijō-kan](/wiki/Daij%C5%8D-kan "Daijō-kan")* included:

* *[Kampaku](/wiki/Kampaku "Kampaku")*, [Fujiwara no Tadahira](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Tadahira "Fujiwara no Tadahira") (藤原忠平), 880–949.[[9]](#cite_note-b296-9)
* *[Daijō-daijin](/wiki/Daij%C5%8D-daijin "Daijō-daijin")*, Fujiwara no Tadahira (藤原忠平).[[9]](#cite_note-b296-9)
* *[Sadaijin](/wiki/Sadaijin "Sadaijin")*, Ono-no Miya [Fujiwara no Saneyori](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Saneyori "Fujiwara no Saneyori") (藤原実頼), 900–970.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* *[Udaijin](/wiki/Udaijin "Udaijin")*, Fujiwara no Saneyori (藤原実頼).
* *Udaijin*, [Fujiwara no Morosuke](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Morosuke "Fujiwara no Morosuke") (藤原師輔), 908–960.[[9]](#cite_note-b296-9)
* *Udaijin*, Fujiwara no Akitada　(藤原顕忠).
* *Udaijin*, [Minamoto no Taka-akira](/w/index.php?title=Minamoto_no_Taka-akira&action=edit&redlink=1 "Minamoto no Taka-akira (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%BA%90%E9%AB%98%E6%98%8E "ja:源高明")]　(源高明).
* *[Nadaijin](/wiki/Nadaijin "Nadaijin")*
* *[Dainagon](/wiki/Dainagon "Dainagon")*

## Eras of Murakami's reign

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Murakami&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Eras of Murakami's reign")]

The years of murakami's reign are more specifically identified by more than one [era name](/wiki/Japanese_era_names "Japanese era names") or *[nengō](/wiki/Neng%C5%8D "Nengō")*.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

* *[Tengyō](/wiki/Tengy%C5%8D "Tengyō")* (938–947)
* *[Tenryaku](/wiki/Tenryaku "Tenryaku")* (947–957)
* *[Tentoku](/wiki/Tentoku "Tentoku")* (957–961)
* *[Ōwa](/wiki/%C5%8Cwa "Ōwa")* (961–964)
* *[Kōhō](/wiki/K%C5%8Dh%C5%8D "Kōhō")* (964–968)

## Consorts and children

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Emperor_Murakami&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Consorts and children")]

[Empress](/wiki/Japanese_empresses "Japanese empresses") (Chugu): [Fujiwara no Anshi](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Anshi "Fujiwara no Anshi")/Yasuko (藤原安子; 927–964), [Fujiwara no Morosuke](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Morosuke "Fujiwara no Morosuke")‘s daughter

* Prince: (946)
* First Daughter: Imperial Princess *Shōshi* (承子内親王; 948–951)
* Second son: Imperial Prince Norihira (憲平親王) later [Emperor Reizei](/wiki/Emperor_Reizei "Emperor Reizei")
* Fourth Son: Imperial Prince Tamehira (為平親王; 952–1010)
* Seventh Daughter: Imperial Princess Sukeko (輔子内親王; 953–992), 32nd [Saiō](/wiki/Sai%C5%8D "Saiō") in [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine") 968–969
* Ninth Daughter: Imperial Princess *Shishi* (資子内親王; 955–1015)
* Seventh Son: Imperial Prince Morihira (守平親王) later [Emperor En'yu](/wiki/Emperor_En%27yu "Emperor En'yu")
* Princess: (962)
* Tenth Daughter: Imperial Princess *Senshi* (選子内親王; 964–1035), 16th [Saiin](/wiki/Saiin_(Priestess) "Saiin (Priestess)") in [Kamo Shrine](/wiki/Kamo_Shrine "Kamo Shrine") 975–1031

Consort ([Nyōgo](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): [Princess *Kishi*](/wiki/Kishi_Jo%C5%8D "Kishi Joō") (徽子女王; 929–985), Imperial Prince Shigeakira's daughter

* Fourth Daughter: Imperial Princess *Kishi*/Noriko (規子内親王; 949–986), 34th [Saiō](/wiki/Sai%C5%8D "Saiō") in [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine") 975–984
* Eighth Son: (962)

Consort ([Nyōgo](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Princess *Sōshi*/Takako (荘子女王; 930–1008), Imperial Prince Yoakira's daughter

* Sixth daughter: Imperial Princess *Rakushi* (楽子内親王; 952–998), 31st [Saiō](/wiki/Sai%C5%8D "Saiō") in [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine") 955–967
* Ninth Son: Imperial Prince Tomohira (具平親王; 964–1009), called *Nochi no Chūshoō* (後中書王)

Consort ([Nyōgo](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no *Jutsushi*/Nobuko (藤原述子; 933–947), [Fujiwara no Saneyori](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Saneyori "Fujiwara no Saneyori")‘s daughter

Consort ([Nyōgo](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no Hōshi (藤原芳子; d. 967), [Fujiwara no Morotada](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Morotada "Fujiwara no Morotada")‘s daughter

* Sixth son: Imperial Prince Masahira (昌平親王; 956–961)
* Tenth son: Imperial Prince Nagahira (永平親王; 965–988)

Court Attendant ([Koui](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Minamoto no Kazuko (源計子), Minamoto no Moroakira's daughter

* Second Daughter: Imperial Princess *Rishi* (理子内親王; 948–960)
* Fifth Daughter: Imperial Princess *Seishi* (盛子内親王; d. 998), married to [Fujiwara no Akimitsu](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Akimitsu "Fujiwara no Akimitsu")

Court Attendant ([Koui](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no Seihi (藤原正妃; d. 967), Fujiwara no Arihira's daughter

* Third Daughter: Imperial Princess *Hoshi* (保子内親王; 949–987), married to [Fujiwara no Kaneie](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Kaneie "Fujiwara no Kaneie")
* Third Prince: Imperial Prince Munehira (致平親王; 951–1041)
* Fifth Prince: Imperial Prince Akihira (昭平親王; 954–1013)

Court Attendant ([Koui](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no Sukehime (藤原祐姫), Fujiwara no Motokata's daughter

* First Son: Imperial Prince Hirohira (広平親王; 950–971)
* Eighth Daughter: Imperial Princess *Shūshi* (緝子内親王; d. 970)

Court Attendant ([Koui](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no *Shūshi* (藤原脩子), Fujiwara no Asahira's daughter

Court Attendant ([Koui](/wiki/K%C5%8Dky%C5%AB "Kōkyū")): Fujiwara no Yūjo (藤原有序), Fujiwara no Arisuke's daughter

Court Lady: Fujiwara no Tōshi/Nariko (藤原登子; d. 975), [Fujiwara no Morosuke](/wiki/Fujiwara_no_Morosuke "Fujiwara no Morosuke")‘s daughter; later married Imperial Prince Shigeakira
